name: Flair NLP

auth.notes: Local library; models auto-download from HuggingFace Hub

performance.notes: GPU recommended; CPU inference usable for moderate throughput

agent_readiness.notes: State-of-the-art NLP library for sequence labeling. Named entity recognition (NER), POS tagging, chunking, text classification. Contextual string embeddings ('Flair embeddings') — character-level language model. Stacked embeddings combining multiple representations. Multi-lingual models. Biomedical NER. Simple API: tagger.predict(sentence). Large model hub. Particularly strong for NER across many languages and domains.

gotchas: Slower than spaCy for inference (richer embeddings = more compute)
Training custom models requires understanding embedding architecture
Less maintained than transformers-based approaches recently
best_when: When Flair's contextual embeddings outperform simpler approaches
Named entity recognition with high accuracy requirements
avoid_when: Simple text classification (SetFit or sklearn better)
summary: Flair is a state-of-the-art NLP framework for NER, POS tagging, and text classification. Famous for 'Flair embeddings' — contextual character-level language models. Multilingual support and biomedical NER models. Simple sentence-level API. Strong NER accuracy across many languages and domains. Slower than spaCy but more accurate for many NER tasks.
